paper_title: Proglacial Peat/Bog Profile Study in Chopta Valley

methods.description: Process for collecting and preparing sediment samples from a proglacial peat/bog profile for isotopic analysis.

steps.description: Samples for isotopic ( 13 C) analysis were collected from a 3m deep sedimentary profile that has been excavated in the proglacial outwash plain.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment samples from a 3m deep profile

steps.description: Sample preparation was done following the procedure discussed in detail in Agrawal et al. and Dubey et al. The samples were dried at room temperature and subsequently powdered to clay size.
steps.searchable_summary: dry and powder sediment samples to clay size

steps.description: 5% HCl solution (three times) was added to the sediments for the removal of carbonates and washed with milli-Q water using a centrifuge machine (~3000 rpm) for the removal of acid and soluble salts.
steps.searchable_summary: remove carbonates and soluble salts from samples using HCl and centrifuge

steps.description: The de-carbonated samples were then dried in a hot air oven with temperature control (< ~45ºC).
steps.searchable_summary: dry de-carbonated samples in a hot air oven

steps.description: The oven dried samples were again powdered with an agate mortar to lose any clumps formed during the earlier processes.
steps.searchable_summary: powder oven-dried samples with agate mortar

methods.description: Procedure for calibrating and modeling the age-depth relationship of sediment samples using R software.

steps.description: Before that all the ages were calibrated using intcal13 and the calibrated ages (2 ) are given in Table S2.
Assuming the modern sample is of 2±1 a, the age-depth was modeled and this is given in Fig. S2.
steps.searchable_summary: model age-depth relationship assuming modern sample age

steps.description: The calibration and the age-depth model were done in R software using Bchron package.
steps.searchable_summary: use Bchron package in R for age-depth modeling
